report_name: RCI Hospitality Discloses Data Breach Resulting from IDOR Vulnerability

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Actor
threat_actors.description: The unauthorized actor responsible for exploiting an IDOR vulnerability on an RCI Hospitality web server to steal contractor data in March 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Steal Personally Identifiable Information (PII) of contractors for potential identity theft or financial fraud.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The actor exploited an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability on a public-facing Microsoft IIS web server to gain unauthorized access to contractor data by manipulating URL parameters.

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
vulnerabilities.description: An access control vulnerability on a Microsoft IIS web server that allowed an unauthorized actor to access contractor records by manipulating URL parameters to cycle through IDs.

identities.name: RCI Hospitality Holdings, Inc.
identities.description: A major operator of nightclubs and sports bars that disclosed a data breach affecting its independent contractors.

identities.name: RCI Internet Services, Inc.
identities.description: Subsidiary of RCI Hospitality Holdings that operated the compromised Microsoft IIS web server.

identities.name: RCI Hospitality Independent Contractors
identities.description: A class of individuals whose Personally Identifiable Information (PII), including names, Social Security Numbers, and driver's license numbers, was exposed in the data breach.

infrastructure.name: RCI Hospitality Contractor Data Web Server
infrastructure.description: A public-facing Microsoft IIS web server belonging to RCI Internet Services, Inc. that was compromised via an IDOR vulnerability, leading to the exposure of contractor PII.

indicators.name: Potential IDOR Probing on JSP Endpoint
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential IDOR Probing on JSP Endpoint
id: 2b9a8f1e-c4d5-4e6f-8a7b-9c0d1e2f3a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects web requests to a JSP endpoint with a numeric ID parameter, which could indicate an attempt to exploit an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability as seen in the RCI Hospitality breach.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/nightclub-giant-rci-hospitality-reports-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-uri-stem|endswith: '/viewProfile.jsp'
    cs-uri-query|re: 'id=\d+'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests to a JSP endpoint with a numeric ID parameter, which could indicate an attempt to exploit an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability.

relationships.target_name: RCI Hospitality Contractor Data Web Server

relationships.source_name: RCI Hospitality Contractor Data Web Server
Potential IDOR Probing on JSP Endpoint
